channel.title: Fajar Lang Blog
channel.description: News, releases, and technical posts from the Fajar Lang project — a statically-typed systems programming language for embedded ML and OS integration.

item.title: Fajar Lang v3.2.0 "Surya Rising" — Edge AI Meets Real Hardware
item.description: Fajar Lang v3.2 bridges the gap between language design and real-world hardware deployment. For the first time, Fajar Lang programs read actual sensor data from a Qualcomm QCS6490 SoC, run ML inference with real neural network weights, and demonstrate production deployment patterns — all from .fj source code. Features real hardware interaction via Linux sysfs, QNN neural network inference with 3 DLC models, multi-model ML pipelines, edge deployment patterns with systemd, and FajarOS Nova with 102 shell commands.

item.title: Fajar Lang on Radxa Dragon Q6A: Embedded ML Meets Real Hardware
item.description: v2.0 "Dawn" marks the first physical hardware deployment of Fajar Lang on the Radxa Dragon Q6A, a Qualcomm QCS6490-based edge AI single-board computer. Covers the cross-compilation pipeline from x86_64 to ARM64, Vulkan GPU compute via Adreno 643, QNN NPU inference on Hexagon 770, and GPIO hardware access — all written in Fajar Lang.

item.title: FajarOS Nova: An OS Written 100% in Fajar Lang, Running on x86_64
item.description: FajarOS Nova is a bare-metal operating system written entirely in Fajar Lang. Nova boots on x86_64 hardware via QEMU, features 97 shell commands, a RAM filesystem, VGA text-mode display, keyboard driver, PCI enumeration, and a preemptive scheduler — all in one language. Every line from the Multiboot2 boot sequence to the shell prompt is Fajar Lang, with no C, no assembly files, and no Rust runtime.

item.title: Introducing Fajar Lang: The Only Language Where OS Kernels and Neural Networks Share One Compiler
item.description: Fajar Lang is a statically-typed systems language for embedded AI where @kernel code can't accidentally call tensor ops and @device code can't accidentally access hardware registers — enforced at compile time. One language, one binary, one type system for flight controllers, ML inference, and mission planning. This post introduces the problem of multi-language embedded AI stacks and how compiler-enforced domain safety solves it.
